definition.name: positive-unital-map
definition.definition: Phi:B(H)->B(H) is positive if Phi(a)>=0 whenever a>=0; positive maps are self-adjointness-preserving, restricting to real-linear Phi:B(H)_sa->B(H)_sa. Unital: Phi(1)=1. A positive unital map is an order-unit contraction: -||a||1<=a<=||a||1 gives -||a||1<=Phi(a)<=||a||1, so ||Phi||=1 on B(H)_sa. [def-positive-unital-map, Idel refs/idel-2013/idel-2013.md:347-348]
